observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

meticulously and judiciously plan projects and programs in order to ensure that these correspond to the most urgent need of the Municipality’s constituents and are crucial to the efficient discharge of governmental functions, in faithful adherence to Section 2 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;

consider updating or amending Executive Order No. 3-B series of 2022 dated February 7, 2022, to identify an accredited FCA in full compliance to relevant terms of the MOA entered into by and between DA Regional Field Office No. 02, DENR Regional Office No. 02 and the Municipality; and 

ensure faithful adherence to all provisions of agreements or contracts executed with other LGUs, national government agencies and other organizations, to efficiently manage the resources of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The SATC was not yet co-managed by an accredited FCA, contrary to duly executed MOA between the Municipality and the Department of Agriculture.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management continue its efforts in enforcing settlements of receivables, and where collection remains unlikely or is no longer feasible, take proactive measures to further enhance financial reporting by:
assessing the impairment of outstanding receivables and recognizing an impairment loss or allowance for probable credit losses as prescribed by IPSAS 41; and
requesting the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2023-008.

We recommended that the Management, through the BAC, ensure transparency, fairness, and uniformity in the procurement process by adhering to the standardized PBDs, properly preparing the bidding documents, and following the general and specific directions of the PBDs to clearly and adequately define the objectives, scope, expected outputs/results of proposed contracts, and the eligibility requirements of bidders.

We recommended the Management, through the BAC and other Municipality Offices, to ensure that all infrastructure projects undertaken by the Municipality are supported by duly approved CSHP in strict compliance with Section 5 of DOLE Department Order No. 13 series of 1998 and Section 37.2.3 (f) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management, through the BAC, strictly observe the prescribed timelines for the preparation, execution, and issuance of procurement documents in compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure the timely delivery of essential services to its constituents.

We recommended that Management, through the offices concerned, ensure strict compliance with the withholding and remittance of taxes, mandatory government deductions and contributions by promptly remitting intact all withheld funds and contributions during the month to the appropriate government agencies by the ensuing month, in faithful compliance with Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and other applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

We commended Management for optimally utilizing the 20% DF and successfully implementing all planned PPAs, contributing to the achievement of the Municipality's desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes in compliance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, dated November 4, 2020, and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, dated February 22, 2017.
We nevertheless recommended that Management continue its efforts to ensure the timely implementation of PPAs for each budget year, and to make efficient use of the identified savings to fund other priority development projects.

We commended Management for the substantial utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with RA No. 10121.
Nevertheless, we recommended that Management, through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO), continue its efforts in ensuring that the PPAs for each budget year funded out of the 5% LDDRMF are well planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready to mitigate, if not totally prevent, delays in the implementation of projects.

We recommended that Management, through the MTO, establish sound internal control over the cash management of the LGU by requiring the DA-SATC Manager to take the following actions to address the observed deficiencies, specifically to:
remit and turn over all collections to the Liquidating Officer on a daily basis to ensure proper safeguarding of funds;
return unutilized cash advances to the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer as soon as they are no longer needed or after prolonged periods of non-use; and
maintain separate cashbooks for collection and cash advance transactions to improve accuracy, monitoring, and reconciliation of each accountability.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, require the Liquidating Officer to strictly observe the daily deposit of collections with the authorized depository bank, in accordance with Section 2 of PD No. 1445 and Section 88 (A), Book II of the LTOM 2nd Edition to prevent the accumulation of significant balances of undeposited cash collections and strengthen safeguards over government funds.

We recommended the Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office, to prepare the necessary entries to adjust the misclassified component items of the PPE accounts, in accordance with the Revise Chart of Accounts for LGUs prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office prepared the necessary journal entry to record the reclassification of the misclassified component items of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC ensure that winning bidders post performance security for the Municipality’s various procurement projects in the form prescribed under Section 39.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to protect its interest in the event of non-compliance with the obligations attached to the contract.

We recommended that:

Henceforth, the Municipal Treasury Office shall prepare and submit in a separate report all advance receipts of taxes from taxpayers to the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate proper accounting thereof in accordance with IPSAS 23 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office and invite a representative of the Schools Division Office/DepEd, to conduct a physical inventory of the aforementioned assets and prepare an Inventory Report containing all information about the existing and serviceable assets to be transferred.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the total accumulated depreciation of PPEs by P295,530.24, and henceforth, adopt the dates of the actual completion/acceptance of Property, Plant and Equipment as basis in the computation of the depreciation expenses as these more reasonably represent the period when assets are available for use in accordance with Paragraph 71 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 17 (IPSAS).

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee carefully perform detailed evaluation during post-qualification of eligible bids to ensure that computational errors and omissions are considered before the execution of the contract between the Municipality and the winning bidder and prevent the incurrence of excessive expenditures as defined under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

Likewise, refund of the excess of the re-calculated bid price over the submitted financial bid proposal of the contractor amounting to P12,540.00 is enjoined.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan consider updating or amending Executive Order No. 3-B series of 2022 dated February 7, 2022 and Resolution No. 2017-27 dated January 18, 2017, respectively, to include certain particulars and provisions which would be in full compliance to relevant terms of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by and between DA RFO No. 02, DENR RO No. 02 and the Municipality.

Also, we recommended that the Municipality ensure faithful adherence to all provisions of agreements or contracts executed with other local government units, national government agencies and other organizations, in order to prevent any risk of not being able to continuously request and receive financial assistance or fund transfers.

We recommended that the BAC perform the detailed evaluation and post-qualification of eligible bids carefully and properly to ensure that computational errors and omissions are considered to enable proper comparison thereof and to avoid the acceptance of bids which exceed the project’s ABC, in faithful adherence to Sections 31.1, 32.2.1(b) and 32.2.4 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

Further, we recommended that the BAC exercise care and prudence in the examination of various eligibility documents submitted by prospective bidders in faithful adherence to relevant provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 to prevent the award of contracts to ineligible contractors and suppliers.

We recommended that the BAC exercise care and prudence in the preparation and issuance of certifications relative to the conduct of various procurement processes required under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 to ensure their propriety and validity.

Also, we recommended that the Municipality ensure faithful adherence to all provisions of agreements or contracts executed with other local government units, national government agencies and other organizations, in order to prevent any risk of not being able to continuously request and receive financial assistance or fund transfers from them.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineering and General Services Offices fast track the procurement and implementation of the programs and projects funded under the 20% Local Development Fund for CY 2022 to ensure that constituents are able to enjoy the benefits that they could derive therefrom.

We recommended that the MDRRMO fast track the implementation of the programs and projects funded under the aforementioned components of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2022 to ensure that the Municipality are given adequate and quality equipment and facilities and other enhanced capabilities to better perform its mandate and allow the constituents to enjoy the benefits that they could derive from it at the soonest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer revisit its collection policies and strategies to identify new measures to improve revenue collection efficiency of the Municipality, particularly on the collection of Real Property Taxes, to fully realize income potentials and provide additional funds to finance socio-economic and development programs and projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Service Officer to undertake the necessary procedures in the titling of the four lots in the name of the Municipality in order to establish ownership in the name of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Legal Officer to issue demand letter to compel the persons liable to settle their audit disallowances immediately in compliance with relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
send demand letters to delinquent borrowers to enforce collection and roll-over the same so that other constituents may be afforded the same opportunity; and
require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile/review the negative balances in the amount of ₱2,000.00 and correspondingly, cause the necessary adjustments to eliminate the same.

6.6 We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to truthfully post every Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award, the Notice of Proceed and the approved contract within the required media and within the specified required time for wider bid opportunities of private contracting parties and for timely information to public of the results of the procurement. 7.3 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Office of the Auditor all perfected contract agreements together with the supporting documents within the required period.

9.3 We recommended that the Management ensure that all payments of contract billings for infrastructure projects are supported with the Certificate of Materials Quality Test or Material Test Results to assure quality of materials introduced in the project, notwithstanding compliance with the documentary requirements prescribed under Item 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
10.5 We recommended that the Management strictly follow the regulation on the ten percent (10%) retention money embodied in Section 6.1 of Annex E of the Revised IRR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strengthen the internal control in the monitoring of cash advances, require the Disbursing Officer to refund immediately any excess cash advances in compliance with Item 5.7 of COA Circular No. 97-002, and require the Municipal Treasurer to utilize the cash advance for the specific purpose for which it was granted in compliance with Item 4.1.7 of same Circular.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Chief Executive has instructed all disbursing officers to utilize all cash advances solely for its intended purpose, and refund any excess without the need of demand upon liquidation of the same and within the timeline as prescribed under the pertinent government circular. 

The Municipal Treasurer has also committed to return any unutilized/over withdrawals for various disbursements right after the consummation of the transaction.

All cash advances, particularly payroll related, were all fully liquidated as evidenced by the zero balance of Advances for Payroll account from January 2019 up December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a mechanism to intensify the collection of Accounts Receivables. We recommended further that Management review the policy in the grant of the accounts receivable to determine the past due accounts and whether there exist the conditions set forth under paragraph 68 of PPSAS 29 (Impairment and Uncollectibility of Financial Assets).
observations.managementAction:
The Management has increased its effort to collect the Accounts Receivable and has been in touch with the Accountable Officers for the settlement of their accounts. From the P1,342,845.02 balance as of December 31, 2018, the LGU was able to collect a total of P924,822.62 as of December 31, 2019, or at a rate of 68.87%.

The Management will re-issue of demand letters to the remaining Accounts Receivable to settle the obligations with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a mechanism to intensify the collection of Loans Receivable. observations.managementAction: The Management has started sending demand letters during the year which resulted to a collection of P372,250.00 as of December 31, 2019. The amount was subsequently loaned to qualified constituents which is the primary purpose of the program, to roll the fund for others to have a seed capital or augment existing fund for their business etc.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the persons liable to settle their audit disallowances immediately in compliance with Item 10.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

This accounts to 42.78% of the total outstanding Receivables as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the LGU post its Quarterly Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality for transparency and accountability purposes as required by Section 5 of Local Budget Memorandum No. 71, 72 and 73 dated September 21, 2015 and March 8, 2016, respectively.

12.6	We recommended that the concerned municipal officials implement the programmed project funded by the share of the municipality from the Burley and Native Tobacco Excise Tax to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to Section 8(b) of Republic Act 8240, and Local Budget Memorandum 69.

13.4	We recommended the LGU’s withholding agent deduct allowable deductions from the employees’ monthly salary following the order of preference prescribed under the aforementioned provisions of 2017 GAA.  If it was determined that succeeding deductions will cause the net take home pay to be lower than ₱4,000.00, this deduction shall no longer be allowed.

14.5	We recommended that management ensure that all disbursements to be made for the grant of financial assistance to various barangays of the Municipality be supported with the documentary requirements prescribed under Sections 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.  Moreover, the details that are important in the implementation/execution of the agreement should be appropriately provided in the Memorandum of Agreements.

15.5 	We recommended that accountable officers secure for the Fidelity Bond in compliance with the provisions of Section 101 of PD 1445 and Treasury Circular 02 – 2009 in order that government may not be put on undue disadvantage when there are defalcations, shortages or unrelieved losses, as the case may be, in the accounts of bonded public officers.

We recommended that the concerned municipal officials be required to submit on time the monthly trial balances,  reports of  collections and deposits, disbursements and checks issued,  including the ORs issued/validated deposits slips and paid payrolls/disbursement vouchers for the auditor’s custody and post audit.
We reiterated our recommendation that management insure with GSIS all the insurable properties of the LGU pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-390.

18.4	We recommended that, since transfer of ownership of the lot is not possible, the management request from the land owner for the annotation in the land title emphasizing the portion of land purchased by the LGU in order that the interest of the LGU over the lot is secured.

We recommended that management exert extra efforts to collect from the recipients of the KKK Livelihood Loan Program. If it can no longer be collected, the Municipal Accountant expedite the documentation for the request  for   write  off   from   the   Commission   on   Audit   (COA)   of   said
receivables. Thereafter, management request authority from the COA to write-off the account pursuant to the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of COA.
